     ARTICLE 10. The recurgo fiscd of two cents on each Ten
Dollars, estabished by the decree of November 8, 1895, in subs-
titution f o r the use of timbres under the law of May 1, 1891,
shall not be affected by this order. It shall be collected as prov-
ided in Article 3 of said decree and shall be in addition to t h e

    ARTICLE 11. This law shall take effect September 10,
1921.
    ARTICLE 12. Executive Order No. 638, and all laws or
parts of laws in conflict with this Order are hereby repealed.

                                  S. S. RORISON,
                        Rear-Admiral, United States Navy,
                       Military Governor of Santo Domingo.

Santo Domingo, D. R.
September 2, 1921.

    MI1,ITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO COMINGO.
                    Executive Order No. 666.
       ADDITIONAL APPROPRIATION TO COVER
          EXPENDITURES UNDER THE LAND
                REGISTRATION ACT.
                   0. G. rJo. 3253.

    By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
of Santo Domingo, thg following Executive Order is hereby pro-
mulgated :
     ARTICLE 1. There is hereby appropriated from funds.
now in the National Treasury not otherwis, appropriated, t h e
sum of TWENTY THOUSAND DOLLARS ($20,000) to cover
expenses of prchasing equipment and supplies, and payment
of salaries in connection with the surveys to be performed under
The Land Registration Act, Executive Order No. 511 as amend-
ed: PROVIDED THAT collections for charges made in ac-
cordance with Executive Orlder No. 511 shall be deposited in the
National Treasury to the credit of the General Account of the

'Treasury.

                                   S. S. ROBISON,
                          Rear-Admiral, United States Navy,.
                         Military Governor of Santo Dorningo.

Santo Domingo, D. R.
September 3, 1921.

     MILITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO DOMINGO.
